task: I’m looking at two used cars this weekend and want to keep my notes organized while I’m at the lot. Can you help me think through a straightforward way to compare the main facts so I don’t miss anything important while I’m checking them out?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — keep it simple and use the same short template for both cars so you can compare them side by side.

### Quick lot-note template
**Car A / Car B**
- **Year / Make / Model / Trim:**
- **VIN / Plate:**
- **Mileage:**
- **Price / fees:**
- **Title status / history report:**

### High-risk checks to note
- **Engine light / dash warnings:** on or off?
- **OBD-II codes:** any stored codes?
- **Tires:** pressure, tread, sidewall damage, uneven wear
- **Fluids:** oil, coolant, washer fluid
- **Leaks under car:** yes / no, where?
- **Battery terminals:** tight / corrosion / loose
- **Lights:** headlights, brake lights, turn signals, reverse lights
- **Brakes / pedal feel:** normal / soft / noisy
- **Strange noises / smells:** yes / no, details

### Condition / paperwork
- **Exterior damage:**
- **Interior wear / odors:**
- **Keys / remotes included:**
- **Service records:**
- **Spare tire / jack:** present? only if you care about longer-trip backup
- **Phone / charger / documents:** with you?

### Simple comparison method
For each car, write:
- **Good**
- **Needs attention**
- **Dealbreaker**

Then at the end, compare only the dealbreakers first.

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page copy/paste checklist** you can use in your phone notes for both cars.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is usable. But I’d want it a little tighter for actually standing in a lot with bad cell service and some salesman talking over me.

Make it into a table or a two-column checklist, one line per item, so I can mark Car A vs Car B fast. Also add a few things that matter more than generic stuff:

- **Cold start:** starts clean / rough / delayed
- **Transmission feel:** shifts clean / hard / slips
- **Steering / alignment:** pulls left/right, wheel off-center
- **A/C and heat:** works or not
- **Rust / underbody:** yes/no, where
- **Check engine status after test drive:** on/off
- **Any recent repairs:** what, when, who did them

And I’d want a final line for each car:
- **Best thing about it:**
- **Worst thing about it:**
- **Would I trust it for daily use? yes / no / maybe**

If you can turn it into a clean note template I can paste straight into my phone, that’d be better than the paragraph version.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a clean **paste-into-notes** version you can use at the lot.

## Used Car Lot Comparison

| Item | Car A | Car B |
|---|---|---|
| Year / Make / Model / Trim |  |  |
| VIN / Plate |  |  |
| Mileage |  |  |
| Price / fees |  |  |
| Title status / history report |  |  |
| Check engine light |  |  |
| OBD-II codes |  |  |
| Cold start | clean / rough / delayed | clean / rough / delayed |
| Idle quality | smooth / rough / stalls | smooth / rough / stalls |
| Transmission feel | clean / hard / slips | clean / hard / slips |
| Steering / alignment | straight / pulls / wheel off-center | straight / pulls / wheel off-center |
| Brakes / pedal feel | normal / soft / noisy | normal / soft / noisy |
| A/C and heat | works / weak / no | works / weak / no |
| Tires | good / worn / uneven / damaged | good / worn / uneven / damaged |
| Rust / underbody | none / some / bad, where: ____ | none / some / bad, where: ____ |
| Fluids | oil / coolant / leaks / low | oil / coolant / leaks / low |
| Battery / terminals | good / corrosion / loose | good / corrosion / loose |
| Lights | all work / issue: ____ | all work / issue: ____ |
| Strange noises / smells | none / yes, details: ____ | none / yes, details: ____ |
| Any recent repairs | what / when / who did it: ____ | what / when / who did it: ____ |
| Service records | yes / no / partial | yes / no / partial |
| Best thing about it |  |  |
| Worst thing about it |  |  |
| Would I trust it for daily use? | yes / no / maybe | yes / no / maybe |

## Quick decision line
- **Car A dealbreakers:**  
- **Car B dealbreakers:**  
- **Winner right now:**  

If you want, I can also make this into a **shorter ultra-fast version** with only the 10 most important checks for when you’re rushed.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s fine. Clean enough to use as-is. The two-column setup is what I needed.
